name: Redtrail Documentation Site
description: Documentation site using Astro Starlight with Catppuccin theming, terminal frame components, hands-on Docker labs, and bilingual content (en/es)

userStories.title: Initialize Astro Starlight project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs-site/ directory initialized with Astro Starlight so that I have a working foundation to build the documentation site on.
acceptanceCriteria: Astro Starlight project initialized in docs-site/ with package.json, astro.config.mjs, and tsconfig.json
Sidebar configured with all 8 content sections: Getting Started, Core Concepts, Guides, CLI Reference, Skills, Configuration, Contributing
A placeholder index page exists and renders
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/
npm run dev starts a local dev server that serves the site

userStories.title: Configure Catppuccin Mocha/Latte theming
userStories.description: As a user, I want the site themed with Catppuccin Mocha (dark) and Latte (light) with red primary accent and green secondary accent, so that the site looks modern and on-brand.
acceptanceCriteria: Starlight CSS custom properties overridden with Catppuccin Mocha hex values for dark mode
Starlight CSS custom properties overridden with Catppuccin Latte hex values for light mode
Red (Catppuccin red) used as primary accent color on links and highlights
Green (Catppuccin green) used as secondary accent on success-styled elements
Dark/light toggle works and persists user preference
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Set up i18n for English and Spanish
userStories.description: As a Spanish-speaking user, I want the site available in both English and Spanish so that I can read documentation in my native language.
acceptanceCriteria: Starlight i18n configured with English as default locale and Spanish as secondary
Directory structure uses src/content/docs/en/ and src/content/docs/es/
Placeholder index page exists in both /en/ and /es/ routes
Language switcher is visible in the site header
Both locale routes resolve and render correctly
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Create GitHub Actions workflow for Pages deploy
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs site to automatically build and deploy to GitHub Pages on push to main, so that the live site stays up to date.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/docs.yml workflow file exists
Workflow triggers on push to main
Workflow installs dependencies, runs npm run build in docs-site/
Workflow deploys the built output to GitHub Pages
Workflow only runs when docs-site/ or .github/workflows/docs.yml files change (path filter)
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Set up Vitest for component testing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Vitest configured in docs-site/ so that I can write and run unit tests for custom Astro components.
acceptanceCriteria: Vitest installed and configured in docs-site/ with Astro component testing support
npx vitest run executes successfully (even with zero tests)
A sample smoke test exists to verify the setup works
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/
npx vitest run passes

userStories.title: Build Terminal Frame MDX component
userStories.description: As a user, I want code examples displayed in a terminal-like UI so that I can clearly see what commands to run and what output to expect.
acceptanceCriteria: Terminal Frame MDX component exists in docs-site/src/components/
Component accepts props: title (window title), prompt (prompt string), content (command + output)
Component renders terminal chrome: title bar with traffic light dots, rounded corners, subtle shadow
Component applies Catppuccin Mocha styles in dark mode and Latte styles in light mode
Component handles edge cases: empty content, very long output, multiline commands
A demo page exists in the docs showcasing the component with various configurations
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Write Vitest tests for Terminal Frame component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the Terminal Frame component so that regressions are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover rendering with all prop combinations (title, prompt, content)
Tests verify theme-aware class application for Mocha and Latte
Tests cover edge cases: empty content, long output, multiline commands
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/
npx vitest run passes

userStories.title: Build Hero landing page component
userStories.description: As a user, I want a visually appealing landing page that communicates what RT does and lets me self-select a learning path based on my experience level.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom Hero component replaces the default Starlight index page
Text logo 'redtrail' displayed prominently in the hero section
One-line value proposition rendered below the logo
Terminal Frame component embedded showing a brief realistic RT session snippet
Three audience path cards rendered: Quick Start (experienced), Understand the Methodology (intermediate), Hands-On Tutorial (beginners)
Each card has a short description and links to the correct section
Links point to locale-prefixed routes (/en/getting-started/, /es/getting-started/, etc.)
Hero content is localized — English and Spanish versions render correctly
Responsive layout works on mobile and desktop viewports
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Write Vitest tests for Hero component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the Hero component so that the landing page rendering and navigation are validated automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests verify three audience path cards render with correct labels
Tests verify links point to correct locale-prefixed routes
Tests verify hero renders in both English and Spanish contexts
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/
npx vitest run passes

userStories.title: Write Getting Started guide in English
userStories.description: As an experienced pentester, I want a quick start guide that shows me the RT workflow in under 10 minutes so that I can decide if the tool is worth adopting.
acceptanceCriteria: Getting Started page exists at /en/getting-started/
Covers full flow: install → rt setup → rt init → recon → rt kb → rt hypothesis → rt ask → rt report
Every RT command example uses the Terminal Frame component with realistic output
Assumes reader knows pentesting but not RT — no hand-holding on external tools
Ends with a Next Steps section linking to Core Concepts and Tutorials
Completable in under 10 minutes of reading
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Translate Getting Started guide to Spanish
userStories.description: As a Spanish-speaking user, I want the Getting Started guide in Spanish so that I can learn the RT workflow in my native language.
acceptanceCriteria: Getting Started page exists at /es/getting-started/
Full translation of the English Getting Started content
Terminal Frame component examples preserved (commands stay in English, descriptions in Spanish)
Next Steps links point to /es/ prefixed routes
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Build simple lab web application
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the vulnerable web application for the simple lab so that users have a realistic target to practice against.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimal web application (Flask or Express) in docs-site/labs/simple/
Login page with default admin credentials (admin/admin or similar)
Admin panel accessible after login with a command injection vulnerability in a form field
A flag file readable only through the command injection → shell path
Application serves on port 80

userStories.title: Configure simple lab Docker Compose with FTP, SSH, and HTTP
userStories.description: As a user, I want to spin up the simple lab with docker compose up so that I can practice RT commands against a real target.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml in docs-site/labs/simple/ defines a single container
HTTP service responds on port 80 with the vulnerable web app
FTP service allows anonymous login and lists decoy files (backup.sql.gz with junk, credentials.txt with fake/expired creds)
SSH service accepts connections
docker compose up boots the environment successfully
docker compose down cleanly tears down
README.md documents setup, teardown, and resource requirements

userStories.title: Write simple lab smoke tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want smoke tests for the simple lab so that I know the environment works as designed.
acceptanceCriteria: Smoke tests verify container boots successfully
Tests verify HTTP returns 200 on port 80
Tests verify FTP allows anonymous login and lists files
Tests verify SSH accepts connections
Tests can run in CI
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Write simple lab tutorial in English
userStories.description: As a beginner, I want a step-by-step tutorial walking through the simple lab with RT so that I can learn both pentesting methodology and RT simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: Tutorial page exists at /en/guides/simple-lab/
Starts with docker compose up to spin up the lab
Walks through: rt init → nmap recon → ingest results → discover services
FTP red herring section: form hypothesis → inspect decoy files → add evidence → refute hypothesis
Real path: discover default web creds → access admin panel → find command injection → exploit → capture flag
Ends with rt report to generate the engagement narrative
Every RT command shown with Terminal Frame component and expected output
Explains the why at each step — not just the commands
Completable in ~30 minutes hands-on
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Translate simple lab tutorial to Spanish
userStories.description: As a Spanish-speaking beginner, I want the simple lab tutorial in Spanish so that I can follow along in my native language.
acceptanceCriteria: Tutorial page exists at /es/guides/simple-lab/
Full translation of the English tutorial content
Terminal Frame examples preserved (commands in English, explanations in Spanish)
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Create English stub pages for remaining sections
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all documentation sections in the sidebar even if not yet written, so that I know the full scope of what the docs will cover.
acceptanceCriteria: Core Concepts stub at /en/core-concepts/ with description of workspace model, KB, hypotheses, deductive layers, BISCL
CLI Reference stub at /en/cli-reference/ listing all rt subcommands with one-line descriptions from --help
Skills stub at /en/skills/ with overview of skill system, built-in list, custom development
Configuration stub at /en/configuration/ with description of global config, workspace config, LLM providers
Contributing stub at /en/contributing/ with architecture overview placeholder
Each stub has a clear Coming Soon indicator
All stubs appear correctly in sidebar navigation
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Translate stub pages to Spanish
userStories.description: As a Spanish-speaking user, I want the stub pages available in Spanish so that the site feels complete in both languages.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 stub pages translated and available under /es/ routes
Coming Soon indicators translated
Sidebar navigation correct in Spanish locale
npm run build succeeds in docs-site/

userStories.title: Build web01 container for complex lab
userStories.description: As a user working through the complex lab, I want web01 to be a deep multi-step challenge with red herrings so that I experience the full depth of hypothesis-driven testing.
acceptanceCriteria: Web application with a hidden admin panel discoverable via directory enumeration
Main application has a stored XSS vulnerability
A bot/script periodically visits pages with a valid admin session cookie (simulates victim for XSS exfiltration)
Admin panel accessible with stolen cookie has RCE via file upload or template rendering
Red herrings present: outdated version header suggesting inapplicable CVE, /api/debug endpoint returning nothing useful, misleading robots.txt
Dockerfile builds successfully

userStories.title: Build app01 container for complex lab
userStories.description: As a user working through the complex lab, I want to pivot from web01 to app01 using reused credentials and find a command injection vulnerability.
acceptanceCriteria: SSH service accepts the same credentials found on web01
HTTP application on port 8080 with a command injection in search or export feature
Config file on disk contains plaintext database credentials for db01
Dockerfile builds successfully

userStories.title: Build db01 container for complex lab
userStories.description: As a user working through the complex lab, I want to access db01 using credentials from app01 and find dc01 admin credentials in a database dump.
acceptanceCriteria: MySQL service accepts credentials found in app01's config file
Database contains a users table with dc01 admin credentials in plaintext or easily crackable hash
File-read privilege escalation path exists (LOAD_FILE or similar)
Dockerfile builds successfully

userStories.title: Build dc01 container for complex lab
userStories.description: As a user working through the complex lab, I want dc01 to be the final target where credentials from db01 grant admin access to capture the flag.
acceptanceCriteria: SSH service accepts credentials found in db01's user dump
Flag file readable only with admin access
Dockerfile builds successfully

userStories.title: Configure complex lab Docker Compose networking
userStories.description: As a user, I want the complex lab to simulate a corporate network topology where only the DMZ host is directly reachable from my machine.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml in docs-site/labs/complex/ defines all 4 containers
Two Docker networks: external (host-accessible) and internal (isolated)
web01 connected to both networks (DMZ pivot point)
app01, db01, dc01 connected only to internal network — not reachable from host
Health checks ensure services are ready before dependent containers start
docker compose up boots all containers and networks successfully
docker compose down cleanly tears down everything
README documents topology diagram, setup, teardown, and resource requirements

userStories.title: Write complex lab smoke tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want smoke tests for the complex lab so that I know the full attack chain works as designed.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests verify all 4 containers boot successfully
Tests verify web01 HTTP responds from host, app01/db01/dc01 are not directly reachable
Tests verify web01 services: HTTP 200, hidden admin panel exists, XSS injectable
Tests verify app01 SSH accepts expected credentials, HTTP responds on 8080
Tests verify db01 MySQL accepts expected credentials
Tests verify dc01 SSH accepts expected credentials and flag is readable
Tests can run in CI
